name: getLuminance
description: Returns the global landscape luminance value (0.0 to 1.0). Returns 1 if atmosphere drawing is on and no eclipse is underway, 0 if atmosphere is switched off. This value is used for eye adaptation calculations.

name: getAtmosphereAverageLuminance
description: Returns the average luminance of the atmosphere in candela per square meter (cd/m²). Typical values: ~10 at sunset, ~6400 in daylight, >0 in dark night.

name: setAtmosphereAverageLuminance
description: Overrides the auto-computed average luminance of the atmosphere. Useful for creating images of adjacent sky regions with the same brightness, or for simulating over/underexposure. Call with a negative value to unfreeze.

parameters.name: overrideLuminance
parameters.description: Luminance value in cd/m² to override (negative value to unfreeze)

name: getAllLandscapeNames
description: Returns a list of display names for all available landscapes (from landscape.ini files).

name: getAllLandscapeIDs
description: Returns a list of unique identifiers (directory names) for all available landscapes.
example:
var ids = LandscapeMgr.getAllLandscapeIDs();
core.debug("Landscape IDs: " + ids.join(", "));

name: getUserLandscapeIDs
description: Returns a list of identifiers for user-installed landscapes (excluding those packaged with Stellarium).

name: getCurrentLandscapeID
description: Returns the unique identifier (directory name) of the currently loaded landscape.
example:
var landscapeId = LandscapeMgr.getCurrentLandscapeID();
core.debug("Current landscape ID: " + landscapeId);

name: setCurrentLandscapeID
description: Changes the current landscape to the landscape with the specified ID. If the landscape has location data and flagLandscapeSetsLocation is enabled, the observer location will also change.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Landscape ID (directory name, e.g., 'guereins', 'ocean', 'zero')

parameters.name: changeLocationDuration
parameters.description: Duration of the location transition animation in seconds

returnDescription: true if landscape was successfully loaded, false otherwise

name: getCurrentLandscapeName
description: Returns the display name of the currently loaded landscape as defined in its landscape.ini file.
example:
var landscapeName = LandscapeMgr.getCurrentLandscapeName();
core.debug("Current landscape: " + landscapeName);

name: setCurrentLandscapeName
description: Changes the current landscape by its display name (as shown in the GUI).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Landscape display name (e.g., 'Ocean', 'Guereins', 'Zero Horizon')

parameters.name: changeLocationDuration
parameters.description: Duration of the location transition animation in seconds

returnDescription: true if landscape was successfully loaded, false otherwise

name: getCurrentLandscapeBrightness
description: Returns the current brightness value of the landscape (0.0 to 1.0). Can also retrieve the light layer brightness (illumination overlay).

parameters.name: light
parameters.description: If true, retrieves the light layer brightness; if false, retrieves the landscape brightness

name: getDefaultLandscapeID
description: Returns the ID of the default landscape that loads when Stellarium starts.
example:
var defaultLandscape = LandscapeMgr.getDefaultLandscapeID();
core.debug("Default landscape: " + defaultLandscape);

name: setDefaultLandscapeID
description: Sets the default landscape that will load when Stellarium starts. The setting is saved to the configuration file.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Landscape ID to use as default

returnDescription: true if successfully set, false otherwise

name: getCurrentLandscapeHtmlDescription
description: Returns an HTML-formatted description of the current landscape including author, location, atmospheric conditions, and light pollution information.

name: getDescription
description: Returns the description text of the current landscape from either a localized description file or the landscape.ini file.
example:
var desc = LandscapeMgr.getDescription();
core.debug("Landscape description: " + desc);

name: getCacheSize
description: Returns the maximum size of the landscape cache in megabytes. The cache stores preloaded landscapes for faster switching.
example:
var cacheSize = LandscapeMgr.getCacheSize();
core.debug("Landscape cache size: " + cacheSize + " MB");

name: setCacheSize
description: Sets the maximum size of the landscape cache in megabytes. Larger cache allows more landscapes to be kept in memory for faster switching.

parameters.name: mb
parameters.description: Cache size in megabytes (default: 100, modern systems can use 500-1000)

name: getCacheFilledSize
description: Returns the current amount of memory used by the landscape cache in megabytes (approximate).

name: getCacheCount
description: Returns the number of landscapes currently stored in the cache.
example:
var cachedLandscapes = LandscapeMgr.getCacheCount();
core.debug("Landscapes in cache: " + cachedLandscapes);

name: precacheLandscape
description: Preloads a landscape into the cache for faster access later. Useful for smooth transitions between landscapes in presentations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Landscape ID to preload

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: If true, replaces existing cached version; if false and already cached, does nothing

returnDescription: true if successfully cached, false otherwise

name: removeCachedLandscape
description: Removes a landscape from the cache to free memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Landscape ID to remove from cache

returnDescription: true if successfully removed, false if not found

name: getFlagLandscape
description: Returns whether the landscape (ground and scenery) is currently visible.
returnDescription: true if landscape is visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagLandscape
description: Shows or hides the landscape (ground and scenery). Hiding the landscape makes the sky visible all the way down to the mathematical horizon.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show landscape, false to hide

name: getIsLandscapeFullyVisible
description: Returns whether the landscape is currently fully visible (opaque). Useful for determining if objects below the landscape should be rendered.
returnDescription: true if landscape is fully visible, false if fading or hidden

name: getLandscapeSinMinAltitudeLimit
description: Returns the sine of the minimum altitude angle that the landscape covers. Can be used to construct bounding caps for culling stars below the horizon.
returnDescription: Sine of minimum landscape altitude (default: -0.035, i.e., sin(-2 degrees))

name: getFlagFog
description: Returns whether the fog effect is visible on the horizon. Fog is only drawn when atmosphere is also enabled.

name: setFlagFog
description: Shows or hides the fog effect on the horizon. Adds realism to distant horizons.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show fog, false to hide

name: getFlagIllumination
description: Returns whether the illumination layer (light pollution, city lights, windows) is visible on the landscape.
returnDescription: true if illumination layer is visible

name: setFlagIllumination
description: Shows or hides the illumination layer on the landscape (lights, light pollution, etc.). Creates a glowing cityscape effect at night.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show illumination layer, false to hide

name: getFlagLabels
description: Returns whether landscape feature labels (mountain names, landmarks, etc.) are visible.
returnDescription: true if landscape labels are visible

name: setFlagLabels
description: Shows or hides landscape feature labels (mountain names, landmarks, etc.).

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show labels, false to hide

name: getLabelFontSize
description: Returns the font size of landscape feature labels in pixels.
example:
var fontSize = LandscapeMgr.getLabelFontSize();
core.debug("Label font size: " + fontSize + " px");

name: setLabelFontSize
description: Sets the font size of landscape feature labels in pixels.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Font size in pixels (recommended: 12-24)

name: getLabelAngle
description: Returns the rotation angle of landscape feature labels in degrees.
example:
var angle = LandscapeMgr.getLabelAngle();
core.debug("Label rotation angle: " + angle + "°");

name: setLabelAngle
description: Sets the rotation angle of landscape feature labels in degrees. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise.

parameters.name: angleDeg
parameters.description: Rotation angle in degrees (recommended: 0 to 90)

name: getLabelColor
description: Returns the color of landscape feature labels.
example:
var color = LandscapeMgr.getLabelColor();
core.debug("Label color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");

name: setLabelColor
description: Sets the color of landscape feature labels.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector

name: getLandscapeTransparency
description: Returns the transparency level of the landscape (0.0 = fully opaque, 1.0 = fully transparent).
example:
var transparency = LandscapeMgr.getLandscapeTransparency();
core.debug("Landscape transparency: " + transparency);

name: setLandscapeTransparency
description: Sets the transparency level of the landscape. Useful for seeing stars through the landscape or creating ghost effects.

parameters.name: transparency
parameters.description: Transparency value (0.0 = opaque, 0.5 = semi-transparent, 1.0 = fully transparent)

name: getFlagLandscapeUseTransparency
description: Returns whether landscape transparency is enabled.

name: setFlagLandscapeUseTransparency
description: Enables or disables landscape transparency. Must be enabled for setLandscapeTransparency to have effect.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable transparency, false to disable

name: getFlagPolyLineOnlyDisplayed
description: Returns whether only the polygonal horizon line is displayed (without the landscape texture). Useful for debugging or when only horizon line is needed.
returnDescription: true if only polyline is displayed

name: setFlagPolyLineOnlyDisplayed
description: Sets whether only the polygonal horizon line should be displayed (without the landscape texture).

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to show only the horizon line, false to show full landscape

name: getPolyLineThickness
description: Returns the thickness of the polygonal horizon line in pixels.
example:
var thickness = LandscapeMgr.getPolyLineThickness();
core.debug("Horizon line thickness: " + thickness + " px");

name: setPolyLineThickness
description: Sets the thickness of the polygonal horizon line in pixels.

parameters.name: thickness
parameters.description: Line thickness in pixels (1 to 5, default: 1)

name: getPolyLineColor
description: Returns the color of the polygonal horizon line.
example:
var color = LandscapeMgr.getPolyLineColor();
core.debug("Polyline color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");

name: setPolyLineColor
description: Sets the color of the polygonal horizon line.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector

name: getFlagLandscapeUseMinimalBrightness
description: Returns whether minimal brightness is enforced to prevent the landscape from becoming too dark.
returnDescription: true if minimal brightness is enforced

name: setFlagLandscapeUseMinimalBrightness
description: Sets whether minimal brightness should be enforced to prevent the landscape from becoming too dark.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enforce minimal brightness, false to allow natural darkness

name: getFlagLandscapeSetsMinimalBrightness
description: Returns whether the landscape's own minimal brightness value is used instead of the global default.
returnDescription: true if landscape-specific minimal brightness is used

name: setFlagLandscapeSetsMinimalBrightness
description: Sets whether to use the landscape's own minimal brightness value or the global default.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to use landscape value, false to use global default

name: getDefaultMinimalBrightness
description: Returns the global default minimal brightness value for landscapes (0.0 to 1.0).
example:
var minBright = LandscapeMgr.getDefaultMinimalBrightness();
core.debug("Default minimal brightness: " + minBright);

name: setDefaultMinimalBrightness
description: Sets the global default minimal brightness value for landscapes.

parameters.name: brightness
parameters.description: Minimal brightness value (0.0 to 1.0, default: 0.01)

name: getFlagLandscapeSetsLocation
description: Returns whether changing the landscape automatically updates the observer location to the landscape's associated location.

name: setFlagLandscapeSetsLocation
description: Sets whether changing the landscape should automatically update the observer location.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable location change with landscape, false to disable

name: getFlagUseLightPollutionFromDatabase
description: Returns whether light pollution data is taken from the location database rather than from the landscape or manual settings.
returnDescription: true if using database light pollution data

name: setFlagUseLightPollutionFromDatabase
description: Sets whether to use light pollution data from the location database.

parameters.name: useDatabase
parameters.description: true to use database values, false to use manual/landscape settings

name: increaseLightPollution
description: Increases the light pollution level by moving one step up the Bortle scale (1-9). Useful for quick adjustments.

name: reduceLightPollution
description: Reduces the light pollution level by moving one step down the Bortle scale (1-9).

name: cyclicChangeLightPollution
description: Cycles through all light pollution levels (1 to 9 repeatedly). Useful for demonstrations.

name: getFlagCardinalPoints
description: Returns whether the cardinal points (N, S, E, W) are displayed on the horizon. These are the four main compass directions.
returnDescription: true if cardinal points are visible

name: setFlagCardinalPoints
description: Shows or hides the cardinal points (N, S, E, W) on the horizon.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show cardinal points, false to hide

name: getFlagOrdinalPoints
description: Returns whether the ordinal (intercardinal) points (NE, SE, SW, NW) are displayed.
returnDescription: true if ordinal points are visible

name: setFlagOrdinalPoints
description: Shows or hides the ordinal (intercardinal) points (NE, SE, SW, NW).

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show ordinal points, false to hide

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagOrdinalPoints(true);

name: getFlagOrdinal16WRPoints
description: Returns whether the secondary intercardinal points (16-wind compass rose: NNE, ENE, ESE, SSE, SSW, WSW, WNW, NNW) are displayed.

name: setFlagOrdinal16WRPoints
description: Shows or hides the secondary intercardinal points (16-wind compass rose).

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show 16-wind points, false to hide

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagOrdinal16WRPoints(true);

name: getFlagOrdinal32WRPoints
description: Returns whether the tertiary intercardinal points (32-wind compass rose with points like NbE, NEbN, etc.) are displayed.

name: setFlagOrdinal32WRPoints
description: Shows or hides the tertiary intercardinal points (32-wind compass rose).

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show 32-wind points, false to hide

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagOrdinal32WRPoints(true);

name: getColorCardinalPoints
description: Returns the current color of the cardinal and ordinal point labels.
example:
var color = LandscapeMgr.getColorCardinalPoints();
core.debug("Cardinal points color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");

name: setColorCardinalPoints
description: Sets the color of the cardinal and ordinal point labels.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0

name: getFlagAtmosphere
description: Returns whether atmosphere rendering is currently enabled.
returnDescription: true if atmosphere is visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagAtmosphere
description: Shows or hides the atmosphere rendering. When disabled, the sky appears black (like in space) but atmospheric refraction and extinction may still be active.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to show atmosphere, false to hide

name: getAtmosphereModel
description: Returns the current atmosphere model name. Options: 'preetham' (default, fast) or 'showmysky' (physically based, more realistic).
example:
var model = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereModel();
core.debug("Current atmosphere model: " + model);

name: setAtmosphereModel
description: Sets the atmosphere model to use. 'preetham' is the classic fast model. 'showmysky' is a physically based model requiring Qt6 and data files.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Model name: 'preetham' or 'showmysky'

name: getAtmosphereModelPath
description: Returns the file system path where atmosphere model data files are stored.
returnDescription: Path to atmosphere model data directory
example:
var path = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereModelPath();
core.debug("Atmosphere data path: " + path);

name: setAtmosphereModelPath
description: Sets the file system path where atmosphere model data files are stored. Useful for custom ShowMySky installations.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Directory path containing atmosphere model data

example:
// Set custom path for ShowMySky data
// LandscapeMgr.setAtmosphereModelPath("/path/to/showmysky/data");

name: getDefaultAtmosphereModelPath
description: Returns the default file system path for atmosphere model data files (inside Stellarium installation directory).
example:
var defaultPath = LandscapeMgr.getDefaultAtmosphereModelPath();
core.debug("Default atmosphere path: " + defaultPath);

name: getAtmosphereShowMySkyStoppedWithError
description: Returns whether the ShowMySky atmosphere model has stopped due to an error (e.g., missing data files or incompatible hardware).
returnDescription: true if ShowMySky encountered an error and stopped

name: getAtmosphereShowMySkyStatusText
description: Returns the current status text of the ShowMySky atmosphere model, showing loading progress or error messages.
example:
var status = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereShowMySkyStatusText();
core.debug("ShowMySky status: " + status);

name: getFlagAtmosphereNoScatter
description: Returns whether atmospheric scattering (blue sky effect) is disabled while other effects (refraction, extinction) remain active. Useful for debugging or special visual effects.
example:
var noScatter = LandscapeMgr.getFlagAtmosphereNoScatter();
core.debug("Atmosphere scattering disabled: " + noScatter);

name: setFlagAtmosphereNoScatter
description: Enables or disables atmospheric scattering (blue sky effect) while keeping refraction and extinction active.

parameters.name: displayed
parameters.description: true to disable scattering, false to enable normal scattering

name: getAtmosphereFadeIntensity
description: Returns the current fade intensity of the atmosphere (0.0 = fully faded out, 1.0 = fully visible). Used for smooth transitions.
example:
var fade = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereFadeIntensity();
core.debug("Atmosphere fade: " + fade);

name: getAtmosphereFadeDuration
description: Returns the fade duration of the atmosphere in seconds. Longer durations create smoother transitions when toggling atmosphere visibility.
example:
var duration = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereFadeDuration();
core.debug("Atmosphere fade duration: " + duration + " seconds");

name: setAtmosphereFadeDuration
description: Sets the fade duration of the atmosphere in seconds.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Fade duration in seconds (recommended: 0.5 to 3.0)

name: getAtmosphereEclipseSimulationQuality
description: Returns the quality level for eclipse simulation in the atmosphere. Higher quality gives more realistic eclipse appearance but may impact performance.
example:
var quality = LandscapeMgr.getAtmosphereEclipseSimulationQuality();
core.debug("Eclipse quality: " + quality);

name: setAtmosphereEclipseSimulationQuality
description: Sets the quality level for eclipse simulation in the atmosphere.

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Quality level: 0 = low, 1 = medium, 2 = high

name: getFlagAtmosphereZeroOrderScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Returns whether zero-order scattering (light that passes directly through atmosphere without scattering) is enabled.
returnDescription: true if zero-order scattering is enabled

name: setFlagAtmosphereZeroOrderScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Enables or disables zero-order scattering (light that passes directly through atmosphere without scattering).

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable, false to disable

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagAtmosphereZeroOrderScattering(true);

name: getFlagAtmosphereSingleScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Returns whether single-scattering (light scattered exactly once) is enabled.
example:
var singleScatter = LandscapeMgr.getFlagAtmosphereSingleScattering();
core.debug("Single scattering: " + singleScatter);

name: setFlagAtmosphereSingleScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Enables or disables single-scattering (light scattered exactly once).

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable, false to disable

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagAtmosphereSingleScattering(true);

name: getFlagAtmosphereMultipleScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Returns whether multiple-scattering (light scattered multiple times) is enabled. This feature improves realism but is computationally intensive.
example:
var multiScatter = LandscapeMgr.getFlagAtmosphereMultipleScattering();
core.debug("Multiple scattering: " + multiScatter);

name: setFlagAtmosphereMultipleScattering
description: [ShowMySky] Enables or disables multiple-scattering (light scattered multiple times).

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable, false to disable

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagAtmosphereMultipleScattering(true);

name: getFlagAtmospherePseudoMirror
description: [ShowMySky] Returns whether pseudo-mirror effect is enabled for the atmosphere rendering.
example:
var pseudoMirror = LandscapeMgr.getFlagAtmospherePseudoMirror();
core.debug("Pseudo mirror: " + pseudoMirror);

name: setFlagAtmospherePseudoMirror
description: [ShowMySky] Enables or disables pseudo-mirror effect for the atmosphere rendering.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable, false to disable

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagAtmospherePseudoMirror(true);

name: getLandscapeOpacity
description: Returns the opacity of the landscape in a given direction (0 = fully transparent/sky, 1 = fully opaque/landscape). Useful for determining if a celestial object is above or below the true horizon.

parameters.name: azalt
parameters.description: Direction vector in Altitude/Azimuth coordinate frame (normalized)

name: getLandscapeOpacity
description: Returns the opacity of the landscape at a specific azimuth and altitude (both in degrees).

parameters.name: azimuth
parameters.description: Azimuth angle in degrees (0° = North, 90° = East, 180° = South, 270° = West)

parameters.name: altitude
parameters.description: Altitude angle in degrees (0° = horizon, 90° = zenith)

name: setLandscapeTint
description: Sets a color tint for the landscape. Useful for simulating sunrise/sunset colors (red/orange hues) or special lighting effects.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector (R, G, B) with values between 0.0 and 1.0

name: getLandscapeTint
description: Returns the current color tint applied to the landscape.
example:
var tint = LandscapeMgr.getLandscapeTint();
core.debug("Landscape tint: RGB(" + tint[0] + ", " + tint[1] + ", " + tint[2] + ")");

name: getFlagLandscapeAutoSelection
description: Returns whether landscape auto-selection is enabled. When enabled, Stellarium automatically selects an appropriate landscape when moving to a different planet.

name: setFlagLandscapeAutoSelection
description: Enables or disables landscape auto-selection. When enabled, Stellarium automatically selects an appropriate landscape when moving to a different planet.

parameters.name: enableAutoSelect
parameters.description: true to enable auto-selection, false to disable

name: getFlagEnvironmentAutoEnable
description: Returns whether environment auto-enable is active. When enabled, atmosphere and landscape settings automatically adjust when moving between planets.

name: setFlagEnvironmentAutoEnable
description: Enables or disables environment auto-enable. When enabled, atmosphere and landscape settings automatically adjust when moving between planets.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable auto-enable, false to disable

example: LandscapeMgr.setFlagEnvironmentAutoEnable(true);

name: setZRotation
description: Sets an additional rotation of the landscape about the Z-axis (azimuth). Useful for special cases where the landscape may change orientation over time, such as on a rotating ship or vehicle.

parameters.name: degrees
parameters.description: Rotation angle in degrees (added to the base rotation from landscape.ini)

name: installLandscapeFromArchive
signature: QString installLandscapeFromArchive(QString sourceFilePath, bool display, bool forAllUsers)
description: Installs a new landscape from a ZIP archive. The archive should contain a landscape.ini file and all required textures. Returns the installed landscape ID on success.

parameters.name: sourceFilePath
parameters.description: Path to the ZIP archive containing the landscape

parameters.name: display
parameters.description: If true, switches to the newly installed landscape

parameters.name: forAllUsers
parameters.description: If true, installs for all users (may require admin rights) - NOT FULLY IMPLEMENTED

returnDescription: Installed landscape ID, or empty string on failure

name: removeLandscape
description: Removes a user-installed landscape permanently. Packaged landscapes cannot be removed.

parameters.name: landscapeID
parameters.description: ID of the landscape to remove (directory name)

returnDescription: true if successfully removed, false otherwise

name: loadLandscapeName
description: Loads and returns the display name of a landscape from its landscape.ini file without loading the entire landscape.

parameters.name: landscapeID
parameters.description: Landscape ID to query

returnDescription: Landscape display name, or empty string if not found
example:
var name = LandscapeMgr.loadLandscapeName("ocean");
core.debug("Ocean landscape name: " + name);

name: loadLandscapeSize
description: Returns the total file size of a landscape on disk in bytes.

parameters.name: landscapeID
parameters.description: Landscape ID to check

name: showMessage
description: Displays a temporary message on the screen (red text at the bottom). Useful for providing feedback in scripts.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Message text to display

name: clearMessage
description: Clears any temporary message currently displayed on the screen.
example: LandscapeMgr.clearMessage();
